text: This paper investigates the problem of designing fully distributed consensus protocols for multi-agent systems with general linear dynamics under directed communication topologies. A common limitation in existing consensus literature is the reliance on the smallest real part of the nonzero eigenvalues of the Laplacian matrix—a global parameter that requires agents to know the structure of the entire network. To overcome this, we propose an adaptive consensus protocol that utilizes only the agents' internal dynamics and the relative states of their immediate neighbors. We demonstrate that this protocol achieves leader-follower consensus for any directed graph containing a spanning tree rooted at the leader. By eliminating the need for global graph information, the proposed approach is entirely distributed. Furthermore, we extend the framework to address containment control in scenarios involving multiple leaders.
